original text:
The Elizabeth R Media Fund's Commonwealth Digital Challenge for 2019 is now open. Application form available here.
We hope the competition will inspire, and help, young innovators and entrepreneurs to find new ways of improving media through the use of technology.
It was originally established as The Elizabeth R Broadcasting Fund in 1995 to assist the development of broadcasting skills in the Commonwealth with a donation from Buckingham Palace. The donation came from royalties from "Elizabeth R", the 1992 BBC documentary which marked the 40th anniversary of the reign of Queen Elizabeth II.
The Fund has fulfilled an important role for more than 20 years, bringing together Commonwealth broadcasters via a programme of travel bursaries and training opportunities.
However, the media landscape has changed greatly since that ground-breaking Elizabeth R documentary and the Fund is now developing to meet the challenges of the media's digital age.
Thomson Foundation has been charged with re-branding and re-launching the Fund.
It is a fitting choice as the foundation has, for over 55 years, helped raise standards of journalism and communications across the world and has trained journalists and media managers from every Commonwealth country.
For any queries on the Commonwealth Digital Challenge, please email: inquiries@thomsonfoundation.org. For an update on the progress of last year's winner, John Otunga, click here.
Sign up to our newsletter for notification of Thomson Foundation journalism competitions and courses.

original text:
The patient support charity Fertility Network UK has announced two new support groups which have been set up recently in Lancashire/Cumbria and in Hull.
I know many people don't like to think that they might need a "support group" but what is so invaluable about the groups is being able to meet up with other people who are going through similar things and to share experiences. It really can be such a huge relief just to know that there are other people out there finding it hard to be happy when a close friend announces a pregnancy, or who take long detours to avoid the local nursery at pick up and drop off times. Going along to a group is a sign of strength rather than suggesting that you need more support than other people and if you see it that way, it can be a useful - and free - way of helping yourself through treatment.
For more information about the Lancashire/Cumbria group, please email lancashirecumbriagroup314@gmail.com and for the Hull group, you can email fertilitygrouphull@gmail.com.
You can find a list of all the other Fertility Network UK groups on their website here. There really is nothing to lose by going along once and seeing what you think - you may find it helps far more than you expected!
This entry was posted in Advice and support and tagged Cheshire, fertility, Fertility Network UK, fertility support, fertility support Cheshire, fertility support Hull, fertility support Lancashire, Hull, infertility, Lancashire by fertilitymatters. Bookmark the permalink.

original text:
To see the collection of prior postings to the list, visit the Rhas21kernels-devel Archives.
To post a message to all the list members, send email to rhas21kernels-devel@oss.oracle.com.
Subscribe to Rhas21kernels-devel by filling out the following form. You will be sent email requesting confirmation, to prevent others from gratuitously subscribing you. Once confirmation is received, your request will be held for approval by the list moderator. You will be notified of the moderator's decision by email. This is also a private list, which means that the list of members is not available to non-members.

original text:
In addition to offering daily CrossFit classes, we provide a variety of services to our members and non-members!
You don't have to be a member at our gym to come practice yoga with us. We offer a FREE community yoga class once a week, currently on Sundays at 11:15am. If you are visiting ICA for the first time, please email in advance and show up 10 minutes early to fill out a waiver.
Maybe you're interested in CrossFit, but you're not quite ready for group classes? Not a problem! Our coaches can work with you 1-on-1 to get you started on your fitness journey. Personal training is also a great way to work on specific strength or skill goals outside of the group class environment.
Why should I get an InBody scan? Regular weight scales give you one number: your weight. But weight can be misleading! We've all heard that muscle weighs more than fat... so as you build muscle and lose fat, the number on the scale may not respond the way you expect it to. Getting periodic InBody scans will provide you with an accurate picture of your body composition and how it is changing over time.
How much do InBody scans cost? $35/scan for ICA members or $50/scan for non-members. This price includes a discussion and break down of your individual results.
Note: ICA members who are completing a nutrition challenge may purchase a 2-scan bundle for $50. The challenge must be of a defined duration, and scans must be completed immediately before and after to qualify for this discounted rate.
Note: We do not recommend the InBody scan if you have an electronic medical device implanted or if you are pregnant.
ICA offers an 8-week summer teen strength and conditioning program for aspiring young athletes. The athletes meet 3x/week to build strength, power, speed, and mobility. If you are interested in being put on the mailing list for this program, contact John at ironcrossathletics@gmail.com.

original text:
We greatly value the indication of origin on our wines. So we naturally also value knowing the origin of our cannabis. Quality is key.
And that is why we are in constant close contact with our outdoor cultivators. They grow their crops ecologically and in the most shadow-free plots in the region, which makes them perfect as a basis for pure cannabis oil.
A growing number of people is experiencing the benefits of cannabis oil. In order to keep the price of cannabis oil controllable, it is important to scale up its production.
The Weed Beer Foundation helps.
For reasons that are beyond us, Dutch health insurers do not reimburse the costs of medicinal cannabis products. Not every patient is in a position to grow their own.
And that is why our motto is: "If you don't grow for your own use, grow for someone else's." A beautiful gesture!
Would you like to join? That would be great! We will supply premium seeds and professional advice on site. Your plants will enable us to supply premium quality cannabis oil to a greater number of people. As a welcome gift, you will receive a bottle of craft brewed weed beer.
Curious? For more information, send an email to info@sensunique.nu or complete the form below.
